source.description: Six-panel marker process contact sheet generated in Codex image generation and saved under drafts/parade-drum-doodle before cropping.
source.notes: Promoted from a previously generated alternate sheet after owner requested these unused image tutorials be backfilled.

process_strategy: The same drum pose builds from cylinder guides to rims, lugs, crossed sticks, center star, marker fills, and final black outline cleanup.

final_elements.name: round drum shell
final_elements.description: A short cylindrical drum body with a broad top ellipse and curved lower edge.

final_elements.name: top and bottom rims
final_elements.description: Thick bands around the top and bottom of the drum shell.

final_elements.name: side lugs and straps
final_elements.description: Round lug buttons and vertical side straps spaced across the drum.

final_elements.name: crossed drumsticks
final_elements.description: Two rounded sticks crossing over the top of the drum.

final_elements.name: center star badge
final_elements.description: A large star placed on the front center of the drum shell.

final_elements.name: parade marker colors
final_elements.description: Blue shell, gold rims and star, red straps, tan sticks, and visible marker streaks.

final_elements.name: sticker shadow and shine
final_elements.description: Small white highlight gaps and a gray sticker-style shadow around the drum.

frames.visible_job: Places the short drum shell cylinder using an ellipse, side edges, base curve, and center guide.
Adds thick top and bottom rim bands that follow the existing drum cylinder.
Places round lugs and vertical straps across the existing drum front and sides.
Adds two crossed drumsticks over the top rim and a center star badge on the shell.
Adds parade marker colors, visible marker texture, white shine gaps, and sticker shadow to existing shapes.

final_step.visible_job: Only reinforces existing black outlines, deepens marker colors, sharpens highlights, and darkens the sticker shadow.

rejection_checks: No major contour, prop, marking, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No frame may darken, ink, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible in an earlier frame.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
